title: Tuning Knobs vs. Learned Weights
concept: The difference between parameters (weights learned by the model) and hyperparameters (settings you choose before training).

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main goal of training a machine learning model?

options.text: To let the model learn patterns and rules automatically from data.
To manually draw every prediction line by hand.
To memorize only the test data.

exercises.explain: Correct! Training allows a model to analyze data and discover its own internal rules and patterns.

cards.title: The smart race car
cards.body: Imagine you have a self-driving toy car. You can use a knob on the controller to set its speed limit before the race. Once the race starts, the car's computer calculates the best steering angles to stay on the track. You set the limit, but the car learns how to steer!

cards.title: Knobs vs. Learned Weights
cards.body:
In machine learning:
• Hyperparameters (Tuning Knobs) are settings chosen by the human designer before training begins (like max tree depth).
• Parameters (Learned Weights) are the internal values the model learns and adjusts during training (like a prediction line's slope).

cards.title: The baking recipe
cards.body: Think of hyperparameters as the recipe settings: the oven temperature and bake time you set beforehand. Parameters are like the rising cake itself: how much the batter bakes and changes color internally based on those oven settings.

cards.title: Model settings in action
cards.body:
Let's look at two models we know:
• Decision Trees: You set the maximum depth (hyperparameter). The tree learns which specific questions to ask at each split (parameters).
• kNN: You choose the number of neighbors, k (hyperparameter). The model uses this to count local votes.

cards.title: AI doesn't do it all
cards.body:
Myth: An AI model learns absolutely all of its settings automatically from the data.
Reality: The model only learns its internal parameters. Humans must choose the hyperparameters (the knobs) to guide how the learning process happens.

cards.title: Tuning Knobs vs. Weights
cards.body: How settings and learned knowledge compare:

left.text:
Chosen by the human designer.
Set before training starts.
Examples: Max tree depth, k neighbors.

right.text:
Learned automatically by AI.
Adjusted during training.
Examples: Line slope, split questions.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a hyperparameter that a human must set before training starts?

options.text: The maximum depth limit of a Decision Tree
The final mathematical slope of a regression line
The specific questions a tree decides to ask
The actual labels of the test dataset

exercises.explain: Correct! Max depth is a hyperparameter chosen by the designer to control the tree's growth.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The machine learning model learns hyperparameter values by itself during training.
exercises.explain: False! The model only learns internal parameters. Humans must select the hyperparameters (knobs) beforehand.

exercises.sentence: While the model automatically learns internal ___ during training, the designer must set the hyperparameters beforehand.
exercises.explain: Correct! Internal parameters (like weights) are learned by the model, while hyperparameters are set by humans.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a learned parameter (not a hyperparameter)?

options.text: The final slope of a linear regression line
Setting the number of neighbors (k) in kNN to 5
Setting the maximum depth of a Decision Tree to 3

exercises.explain: The slope of a line is calculated and learned by the model during training, making it a parameter.

exercises.prompt: You are building a Decision Tree. To prevent it from memorizing details, you want to limit its size. How do you do this, and what are you adjusting?

options.text: You set the max depth hyperparameter before training, adjusting the learning knob.
You manually edit the tree's learned parameters after training is done.
You let the model learn the max depth by itself because it is a parameter.
You change the training data labels to force the tree to stop growing.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Max depth is a hyperparameter. You set it before training to control how the model learns its parameters. Next, let's see how adjusting these knobs shifts model behavior!
